lede: A look at September 25 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jean-Philippe Rameau and Sandro Pertini, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Maze Prison escape
events.desc: Thirty-eight IRA prisoners, armed with six handguns, hijack a prison meals lorry and smash their way out of the Maze Prison.

events.title: Chicago Marathon
events.desc: About 4,200 people take part in the first running of the Chicago Marathon.

events.title: North Yemen civil war
events.desc: The North Yemen Civil War begins when Abdullah al-Sallal dethrones the newly crowned Imam al-Badr and declares Yemen a republic under his presidency.

events.title: Algeria
events.desc: The People's Democratic Republic of Algeria is formally proclaimed. Ferhat Abbas is elected President of the provisional government.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Surviving elements of the British 1st Airborne Division withdraw from Arnhem via Oosterbeek.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Second Battle of Champagne begins.

events.title: Sequoia National Park
events.desc: The United States Congress establishes Sequoia National Park.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: Ethan Allen surrenders to British forces after attempting to capture Montreal in the Battle of Longue-Pointe during the invasion of Quebec.

events.title: Vasco Núñez de Balboa
events.desc: Spanish explorer Vasco Núñez de Balboa reaches what would become known as the Pacific Ocean.

births.name: Sandro Pertini
births.line: Italian journalist and politician, 7th President of Italy

births.name: William Faulkner
births.line: American novelist and short story writer, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Wangarĩ Maathai, Kenyan environmentalist and activist, Nobel Prize laureate
Jean Françaix, French pianist, composer, and conductor
Leopold III of Belgium, King of the Belgians from 1934 to 1951
Erich Maria Remarque, German-Swiss author and translator
Foxhall P. Keene, American polo player, golfer, and race car driver

observances.text: Christian feast day: Abadir and Iraja and Companions (Coptic Church)
